expectations.text: Characterization tests for existing handler behavior are written first (valid request succeeds, invalid JSON returns 400)
expectations.evidence: No explicit characterization tests. Test file goes directly to validation test functions. No test for pre-existing invalid JSON → 400 behavior.

expectations.text: A test list or plan is created before writing validation tests
expectations.evidence: No test list comment in handler_test.go. No evidence of test planning in transcript.

expectations.text: Validation tests are written BEFORE validation code is added to the handler
expectations.evidence: Transcript flow indicates tests were created before handler modification, consistent with old skill's Iron Law.

expectations.text: A fake or stub UserStore is created for testing (not a mock framework)
expectations.evidence: handler_test.go lines 16-38: fakeUserStore struct implementing db.UserStore interface directly. Identical pattern to with_skill.

expectations.text: Tests cover: invalid email format, empty name, name over 100 chars, password under 8 chars
expectations.evidence: Four dedicated test functions covering all four validation rules. Same test structure as with_skill.

expectations.text: Tests verify the HTTP status code and error message for each validation failure
expectations.evidence: Tests verify StatusBadRequest and that CreateUser was not called, but do NOT verify error message content in response body.

expectations.text: Valid requests still succeed after validation is added
expectations.evidence: TestHandleRegister_CreatesUserForValidInput verifies StatusCreated and correct CreateUser arguments.

expectations.text: Each test tests one validation rule (not a single test checking all validations)
expectations.evidence: Separate test functions for each validation rule.

expectations.text: Validation logic is clean — not deeply nested if/else chains
expectations.evidence: handler.go lines 54-66: validateRegisterRequest with early returns and sentinel errors. Slightly more compact than with_skill (combined name check on one line) but still clean.
